./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6990:./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6825:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2787:docs/reviews/pr200-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7167:  `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol` `ResourcesDeposited`; `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol` `_resolveAction` / `_doDeposit`; ABI JSON.  
./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6991:./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6854:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3684:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.
./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7030:./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7412:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6525:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.
./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7050:./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7843:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8186:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3684:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.

./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7802:./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7072:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5553:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7895:   866	        emit ResourcesDeposited(clanId, cs.clansmanId, w, ir, wh, fi, tick);
./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7803:./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7074:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5686:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.

./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7931:./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7221:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6825:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2787:docs/reviews/pr200-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7167:  `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol` `ResourcesDeposited`; `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol` `_resolveAction` / `_doDeposit`; ABI JSON.  
./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7932:./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7223:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6854:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3684:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.
./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7963:./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7256:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7412:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6525:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.
./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7979:./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7273:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7843:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8186:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3684:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.

./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8383:./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7991:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3684:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.
./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8414:./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8098:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6525:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.
./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8430:./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8161:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8186:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3684:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.

No drift here. Drift is on durations only (M4) and on ResourcesDeposited event shape (M7).

./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8504:./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8535:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7895:   866	        emit ResourcesDeposited(clanId, cs.clansmanId, w, ir, wh, fi, tick);
./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8505:./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8562:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.

./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8974:./docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.

./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9579:`packages/contracts/abi/IClanWorld.json:3319` / `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:536` — The checked-in ABI artifact is stale relative to the interface. Source changed `ResourcesDeposited` to end in `uint32 tick`, added `WallUpgraded/BaseUpgraded/MonumentUpgraded`, and exposes upgrade-cost getters, but the JSON still has the old `ResourcesDeposited(...,uint64 atTick)` shape and lacks the new building functions/events. Event topics differ, so ABI-based indexers/clients will miss or misdecode deposits and cannot call the new getters. Regenerate and commit the ABI from the compiled interface, then add a small ABI parity check.
./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9617:`packages/contracts/abi/IClanWorld.json:3319` / `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:536` — The checked-in ABI artifact is stale relative to the interface. Source changed `ResourcesDeposited` to end in `uint32 tick`, added `WallUpgraded/BaseUpgraded/MonumentUpgraded`, and exposes upgrade-cost getters, but the JSON still has the old `ResourcesDeposited(...,uint64 atTick)` shape and lacks the new building functions/events. Event topics differ, so ABI-based indexers/clients will miss or misdecode deposits and cannot call the new getters. Regenerate and commit the ABI from the compiled interface, then add a small ABI parity check.

./docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3380:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6938:   486	                _markClanDead(clan.clanId, "starvation", tick);
./docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3459:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:60:The real `_resolveAction` checks `_isStarving(clan)` which also reads `_world.currentTick`. Since the simulation loop variable `tick` ranges from `lastSettledTick` to `currentTick`, and starvation can be set during simulation at any intermediate tick, this comparison against `_world.currentTick` is overly permissive. A clan that starts starving at tick 50 would be treated as "starving" for all simulated ticks from 50 onward even if `currentTick` is 100 — which happens to be correct behavior. However, if `starvationStartsAtTick` is set to a tick *after* the current simulation tick but *before* `_world.currentTick` (can't happen in practice since upkeep runs before resolution each tick), this would be wrong.
./docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3552:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3685:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:22:`ClanWorld.sol:~525` overload is `function _markClanDead(uint32 clanId, string memory, uint64 tick, uint32 excludedBanditId)` — the named-by-position-only `string memory` argument is intentional (forge-lint workaround), but it means callers pass `"starvation"` / `"bandit"` / `"unknown"` and that information is dropped on the floor. The emitted `ClanEliminated(clanId, tick)` event has no cause field. If an operator wants to know whether a clan died from starvation vs. bandit, they must replay logs from preceding `BanditAttackResolved` / `ClansmanKilledByBandit` events. Either add a cause field to `ClanEliminated`, or remove the parameter entirely so the API doesn't pretend to track something it discards.
./docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3554:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3694:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:60:The real `_resolveAction` checks `_isStarving(clan)` which also reads `_world.currentTick`. Since the simulation loop variable `tick` ranges from `lastSettledTick` to `currentTick`, and starvation can be set during simulation at any intermediate tick, this comparison against `_world.currentTick` is overly permissive. A clan that starts starving at tick 50 would be treated as "starving" for all simulated ticks from 50 onward even if `currentTick` is 100 — which happens to be correct behavior. However, if `starvationStartsAtTick` is set to a tick *after* the current simulation tick but *before* `_world.currentTick` (can't happen in practice since upkeep runs before resolution each tick), this would be wrong.

./docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3782:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7515:   486	                _markClanDead(clan.clanId, "starvation", tick);
./docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3876:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:22:`ClanWorld.sol:~525` overload is `function _markClanDead(uint32 clanId, string memory, uint64 tick, uint32 excludedBanditId)` — the named-by-position-only `string memory` argument is intentional (forge-lint workaround), but it means callers pass `"starvation"` / `"bandit"` / `"unknown"` and that information is dropped on the floor. The emitted `ClanEliminated(clanId, tick)` event has no cause field. If an operator wants to know whether a clan died from starvation vs. bandit, they must replay logs from preceding `BanditAttackResolved` / `ClansmanKilledByBandit` events. Either add a cause field to `ClanEliminated`, or remove the parameter entirely so the API doesn't pretend to track something it discards.

./docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-opus-4-7.md:37:**Suggested fix:** if `_world.tickSeeds[tick]` (or equivalent) is preserved, use the historical tick's seed. Otherwise, derive the per-tick seed deterministically from `(blockhash anchor, tick)` so lazy settle is reproducible. Same concern likely applies to other lazy-settle RNG sites — worth a one-line audit follow-up.

./docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3557:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3302:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8349:  - Winter starvation replay is using `_isWinterTick(tick)` in both storage settlement and view simulation.
./docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3566:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3312:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:802:+                _markClanDead(clan.clanId, "starvation", tick);
./docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3611:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3380:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6938:   486	                _markClanDead(clan.clanId, "starvation", tick);
./docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3671:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3459:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:60:The real `_resolveAction` checks `_isStarving(clan)` which also reads `_world.currentTick`. Since the simulation loop variable `tick` ranges from `lastSettledTick` to `currentTick`, and starvation can be set during simulation at any intermediate tick, this comparison against `_world.currentTick` is overly permissive. A clan that starts starving at tick 50 would be treated as "starving" for all simulated ticks from 50 onward even if `currentTick` is 100 — which happens to be correct behavior. However, if `starvationStartsAtTick` is set to a tick *after* the current simulation tick but *before* `_world.currentTick` (can't happen in practice since upkeep runs before resolution each tick), this would be wrong.
./docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3752:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3552:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3685:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:22:`ClanWorld.sol:~525` overload is `function _markClanDead(uint32 clanId, string memory, uint64 tick, uint32 excludedBanditId)` — the named-by-position-only `string memory` argument is intentional (forge-lint workaround), but it means callers pass `"starvation"` / `"bandit"` / `"unknown"` and that information is dropped on the floor. The emitted `ClanEliminated(clanId, tick)` event has no cause field. If an operator wants to know whether a clan died from starvation vs. bandit, they must replay logs from preceding `BanditAttackResolved` / `ClansmanKilledByBandit` events. Either add a cause field to `ClanEliminated`, or remove the parameter entirely so the API doesn't pretend to track something it discards.
./docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3754:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3554:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3694:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:60:The real `_resolveAction` checks `_isStarving(clan)` which also reads `_world.currentTick`. Since the simulation loop variable `tick` ranges from `lastSettledTick` to `currentTick`, and starvation can be set during simulation at any intermediate tick, this comparison against `_world.currentTick` is overly permissive. A clan that starts starving at tick 50 would be treated as "starving" for all simulated ticks from 50 onward even if `currentTick` is 100 — which happens to be correct behavior. However, if `starvationStartsAtTick` is set to a tick *after* the current simulation tick but *before* `_world.currentTick` (can't happen in practice since upkeep runs before resolution each tick), this would be wrong.
./docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3872:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3698:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:802:+                _markClanDead(clan.clanId, "starvation", tick);

./docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5652:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:60:The real `_resolveAction` checks `_isStarving(clan)` which also reads `_world.currentTick`. Since the simulation loop variable `tick` ranges from `lastSettledTick` to `currentTick`, and starvation can be set during simulation at any intermediate tick, this comparison against `_world.currentTick` is overly permissive. A clan that starts starving at tick 50 would be treated as "starving" for all simulated ticks from 50 onward even if `currentTick` is 100 — which happens to be correct behavior. However, if `starvationStartsAtTick` is set to a tick *after* the current simulation tick but *before* `_world.currentTick` (can't happen in practice since upkeep runs before resolution each tick), this would be wrong.
./docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5663:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:802:+                _markClanDead(clan.clanId, "starvation", tick);
./docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5710:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6938:   486	                _markClanDead(clan.clanId, "starvation", tick);
./docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5776:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:802:+                _markClanDead(clan.clanId, "starvation", tick);
./docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5841:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7515:   486	                _markClanDead(clan.clanId, "starvation", tick);
./docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5919:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3685:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:22:`ClanWorld.sol:~525` overload is `function _markClanDead(uint32 clanId, string memory, uint64 tick, uint32 excludedBanditId)` — the named-by-position-only `string memory` argument is intentional (forge-lint workaround), but it means callers pass `"starvation"` / `"bandit"` / `"unknown"` and that information is dropped on the floor. The emitted `ClanEliminated(clanId, tick)` event has no cause field. If an operator wants to know whether a clan died from starvation vs. bandit, they must replay logs from preceding `BanditAttackResolved` / `ClansmanKilledByBandit` events. Either add a cause field to `ClanEliminated`, or remove the parameter entirely so the API doesn't pretend to track something it discards.
./docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5921:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3694:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:60:The real `_resolveAction` checks `_isStarving(clan)` which also reads `_world.currentTick`. Since the simulation loop variable `tick` ranges from `lastSettledTick` to `currentTick`, and starvation can be set during simulation at any intermediate tick, this comparison against `_world.currentTick` is overly permissive. A clan that starts starving at tick 50 would be treated as "starving" for all simulated ticks from 50 onward even if `currentTick` is 100 — which happens to be correct behavior. However, if `starvationStartsAtTick` is set to a tick *after* the current simulation tick but *before* `_world.currentTick` (can't happen in practice since upkeep runs before resolution each tick), this would be wrong.

./docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6829:docs/reviews/pr199-synthesis.md:17:| H1 | `ClanWorld.sol` `_processOrder`, `_validate*Upgrade*`, `_settle*Upgrade*` | 5.4 + 5.5 + 4.7 = **3/3** | HIGH | **Upgrade reservations debit vault at submit-time, not at homebase-arrival.** Spec §8 (`clanworld_v4_spec.md:882, :941`): build "not instant at submission time" — checked "if a clansman reaches homebase and attempts a build action." Impl debits during `_processOrder` even before travel completes. Affects upkeep + score + bandit-loot timing while builder is traveling. **Fix:** defer debit to settle-time (when clansman reaches homebase + ACTING tick). |

./docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7612:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11421:- The PR also silently retunes wood gathering/carry economics outside the Phase 8 scope. It changes the cap/yield/crit constants in [IClanWorld.sol:43](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:43) through [IClanWorld.sol:53](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:53), and `_gatherWood` now uses those new values in [ClanWorld.sol:541](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:541) through [ClanWorld.sol:545](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:545). The current v4 docs still specify `WOOD_CAP = 15e18`, `WOOD_BASE_YIELD = 2e18`, `WOOD_CRIT_BONUS = 1e18`, and `WOOD_CRIT_BPS = 2000` in [clanworld_v4_spec.md:417](/home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/01%20Blockchain%20Game%20Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:417) through [clanworld_v4_spec.md:420](/home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/01%20Blockchain%20Game%20Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:420) and [clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:255](/home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/01%20Blockchain%20Game%20Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:255) through [clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:262](/home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/01%20Blockchain%20Game%20Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:262). If this retune is intentional, the spec needs to be updated; if not, this PR is shipping an unrelated economy change.

./docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:11817:docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7934:docs/reviews/pr199-synthesis.md:17:| H1 | `ClanWorld.sol` `_processOrder`, `_validate*Upgrade*`, `_settle*Upgrade*` | 5.4 + 5.5 + 4.7 = **3/3** | HIGH | **Upgrade reservations debit vault at submit-time, not at homebase-arrival.** Spec §8 (`clanworld_v4_spec.md:882, :941`): build "not instant at submission time" — checked "if a clansman reaches homebase and attempts a build action." Impl debits during `_processOrder` even before travel completes. Affects upkeep + score + bandit-loot timing while builder is traveling. **Fix:** defer debit to settle-time (when clansman reaches homebase + ACTING tick). |

./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:162:- `WOOD_BASE_YIELD`: 2e18 → 1e18 (now `WOOD_YIELD_PER_TICK`)
./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:165:- `WOOD_CAP`: 15e18 → 10e18 (renamed `CLANSMAN_CARRY_CAP`, aliased back as `WOOD_CAP`)
./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:169:Two concerns: (a) scope creep — this should have been a separate PR per gitflow conventions, and (b) the `WOOD_CAP = CLANSMAN_CARRY_CAP` aliasing is misleading, since iron/fish/wheat caps remain distinct constants. Either the rename was sloppy or the intent is to converge all carry caps to one number eventually.
./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:171:### M7 — `ResourcesDeposited` event ABI break (uint64 atTick → uint32 tick, field renames) [`IClanWorld.sol:536-544`]
./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:175:event ResourcesDeposited(uint32 indexed clanId, uint32 indexed clansmanId,
./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:176:    uint256 wood, uint256 iron, uint256 wheat, uint256 fish, uint64 atTick);
./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:178:event ResourcesDeposited(uint32 indexed clanId, uint32 indexed clansmanId,
./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:179:    uint256 woodDelta, uint256 ironDelta, uint256 wheatDelta, uint256 fishDelta, uint32 tick);
./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:189:function recordMonumentReachTick(uint32 clanId, uint8 newLevel, uint64 tick) internal {
./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:201:emit WallLevelChanged(clanId, old, clan.wallLevel, tick);
./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:202:emit WallUpgraded(clanId, clan.wallLevel, uint32(tick));
./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:231:- **Spec compliance on §8 cost tables is line-perfect** — wall L1=20w, L2=35w, L3=30w+5i, L4=40w+10i, L5=50w+15i; base L2=40w+20wh through L5=100w+15i+50wh; monument L1=30w+20wh through L6=150w+20i+80wh, L7-10=200w+25i+100wh+1e18 blueprint. No drift here. Drift is on durations only (M4) and on ResourcesDeposited event shape (M7).
./docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-opus-4-7.md:68:event WallDegradedByCold(uint32 indexed clanId, uint8 newWallLevel, uint32 tick);
./docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-opus-4-7.md:69:event ClansmanColdDeath(uint32 indexed clanId, uint32 csId, uint32 tick);
./docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-opus-4-7.md:95:`_markClanDead` (lines 593-594) emits both. They share the same `(clanId, tick)` key; `ClanDied` adds `reason`. Indexers will dedupe but it doubles event-log gas and is a small noise source. Either drop one, or keep `ClanEliminated` only for compatibility with prior consumers and document that.
./docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-opus-4-7.md:106:`_settleClan` runs `_applyUpkeep` first, then the regrow check, then mission settlement (line 387-390). Effect: a clansman returning with 100 wood at tick X cannot save the clan from cold-death at tick X if the vault is short — the wood is still on the clansman when `_applyUpkeep` runs. Probably intentional (turn-order: burn → settle → end-of-tick), but worth a comment.

./docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:60:The real `_resolveAction` checks `_isStarving(clan)` which also reads `_world.currentTick`. Since the simulation loop variable `tick` ranges from `lastSettledTick` to `currentTick`, and starvation can be set during simulation at any intermediate tick, this comparison against `_world.currentTick` is overly permissive. A clan that starts starving at tick 50 would be treated as "starving" for all simulated ticks from 50 onward even if `currentTick` is 100 — which happens to be correct behavior. However, if `starvationStartsAtTick` is set to a tick *after* the current simulation tick but *before* `_world.currentTick` (can't happen in practice since upkeep runs before resolution each tick), this would be wrong.

./docs/reviews/pr250-r2-synthesis.md:22:| H1 | `ClanWorld.sol:431-441` | HIGH | **Pre-winter starvers still die one tick earlier than fresh winter starvers.** The guard `if (winter && starving && clan.starvationStartsAtTick < tick)` defers death for a clan that starts starving on the first winter tick, but not for a clan already starving BEFORE winter. Example: winter starts at tick 110; a pre-winter starver with `starvationStartsAtTick = 105` dies on tick 110, while a fresh winter starver with `starvationStartsAtTick = 110` first dies on tick 111. **The added test missed this** because it sets both clans' `lastSettledTick` after winter has already begun. **Fix:** comparison needs effective winter starvation start, e.g. `max(starvationStartsAtTick, currentWinterStartTick)`. The H2 R1 fix went 80% but missed the edge. |

./docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.
./docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:22:`ClanWorld.sol:~525` overload is `function _markClanDead(uint32 clanId, string memory, uint64 tick, uint32 excludedBanditId)` — the named-by-position-only `string memory` argument is intentional (forge-lint workaround), but it means callers pass `"starvation"` / `"bandit"` / `"unknown"` and that information is dropped on the floor. The emitted `ClanEliminated(clanId, tick)` event has no cause field. If an operator wants to know whether a clan died from starvation vs. bandit, they must replay logs from preceding `BanditAttackResolved` / `ClansmanKilledByBandit` events. Either add a cause field to `ClanEliminated`, or remove the parameter entirely so the API doesn't pretend to track something it discards.
./docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:45:`_tickSeeds[0]` and `_tickSeeds[1]` are both zero at deploy time (no constructor seed). Spawning is gated by `MIN_SPAWN_COOLDOWN_TICKS`, so first spawns can't happen at tick 0–1 anyway, and mission RNG mixes in `(clansmanId, nonce, tick)` which still gives per-mission distinct hashes — but the deterministic-from-zero base is technically attackable by anyone who can pre-image `keccak256(abi.encode(…, 0x0, …))`. Trivial mitigation: seed `_world.currentTickSeed = keccak256(abi.encode(block.prevrandao, address(this)))` in the constructor.

docs/reviews/pr200-r2-synthesis.md:21:| H2 | `ClanWorld.sol:929-945` (`_settleCompletingMissions`) vs `:386-408` (`_settleClan`) | Codex 5.5 = 1/6 (cross-phase: same as P8 H5 + P10) | HIGH | **Heartbeat mission settlement bypasses normal upkeep/cursor advancement.** Heartbeat directly calls `_settleMissionForClansman()` for due missions but does NOT run per-tick `_applyUpkeep()` or advance `clan.lastSettledTick`. Lazy `_settleClan` applies upkeep first. A heartbeat-settled deposit credits the vault, then later `settleClan()` replays earlier ticks from stale cursor with deposited resources already present → retroactive upkeep payment, incorrect starvation outcomes. **Fix:** make heartbeat mission settlement use the same per-tick chronological pipeline; or update `_settleCompletingMissions()` to apply upkeep + advance cursor. Architectural — best handled in concert with Phase 8 R4 H5. |
docs/reviews/pr200-r2-synthesis.md:36:| M5 | Codex 5.4 + Codex 5.5 + Opus 4.6 + Opus 4.7 + Gemini = 5/6 | **`ResourcesDeposited` event field rename `wood/iron/wheat/fish` → `woodDelta/.../atTick`** changes ABI labels (topic-0 unchanged since types unchanged). Subgraphs referencing `event.params.wood` return undefined. Coordinate indexer schema versions. |
docs/reviews/pr200-r2-synthesis.md:44:- L1: `CLANSMAN_CARRY_CAP` naming misleading — only applies to wood; iron/wheat/fish keep separate caps (3/6 noted)

docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:58:+- wood burn = `1e18` wood per base per tick, plus `0.5e18` wood per living clansman per tick

docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2982:docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:5:PR #250 is a substantial Phase-10 cohesion ship: winter schedule, 2× food upkeep + per-clansman wood burn, cold damage → wall degradation → RNG clansman death, wheat plot WinterLocked transitions, and a unified `_markClanDead` helper. The structural shape is good — death routes through `_markClansmanDead`/`_markClanDead`, dead clans short-circuit at `submitClanOrders` and `_settleCompletingMissions`, defenders are cleared, vault resources burn, gold survives. Tests are dense and exercise most happy paths.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2987:docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:179:6. **Spec compliance is solid except for M1 (wood burn).** §A2 winter timing is correctly implemented as a global recurring schedule (PR v4.5 addendum supersedes the per-season-reset older spec). §10 elimination — vault burn yes, gold preservation yes, mission/defender cleanup yes. §10.2/§10.3 cold damage thresholds (every 2) — correct via integer-division-step pattern. §10.4 wheat plot WinterLocked + restart — correct, modulo M2 boundary.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3041:docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:19:Phase 10 ships: winter schedule (10.1) + winter upkeep 2x food + wood burn (10.2) + cold damage walls degrade then RNG clansman death (10.3) + crop transitions WinterLocked (10.4) + clan death (10.5).

docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3946:docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9028:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:5:PR #250 is a substantial Phase-10 cohesion ship: winter schedule, 2× food upkeep + per-clansman wood burn, cold damage → wall degradation → RNG clansman death, wheat plot WinterLocked transitions, and a unified `_markClanDead` helper. The structural shape is good — death routes through `_markClansmanDead`/`_markClanDead`, dead clans short-circuit at `submitClanOrders` and `_settleCompletingMissions`, defenders are cleared, vault resources burn, gold survives. Tests are dense and exercise most happy paths.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3947:docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9032:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:179:6. **Spec compliance is solid except for M1 (wood burn).** §A2 winter timing is correctly implemented as a global recurring schedule (PR v4.5 addendum supersedes the per-season-reset older spec). §10 elimination — vault burn yes, gold preservation yes, mission/defender cleanup yes. §10.2/§10.3 cold damage thresholds (every 2) — correct via integer-division-step pattern. §10.4 wheat plot WinterLocked + restart — correct, modulo M2 boundary.

docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4313:- wood burn = `1e18` wood per base per tick
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4333:If a base cannot pay the required winter wood burn for a tick:
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4571:export interface IChainClient {
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5012:The requested fixes mostly line up in the contract diff: starvation now uses `< tick`, wood burn is base plus per-clansman, winter events are `uint64`, wheat lock clears existing plots, and §7.3 is patched. The more interesting review risk is in the edges around newly derived ABI/view shapes and winter-time minting.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5120:   510	    event ClanColdShortage(uint32 indexed clanId, uint32 tick, uint256 woodShort);
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5130:1210e90 fix(contracts): Phase 10 super-swarm r2 — starvation guard/timing + wood burn per-base + uint64 ABI revert + wheat plot clear (Closes #283-companion) (#287)
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5165:   446	            uint256 woodNeeded = ClanWorldConstants.WINTER_WOOD_BURN_PER_BASE
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5167:   448	            if (clan.vaultWood >= woodNeeded) {
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5168:   449	                clan.vaultWood -= woodNeeded;
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5170:   451	                uint256 woodShort = woodNeeded - clan.vaultWood;
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5176:   457	                emit ClanColdShortage(clan.clanId, _eventTick(tick), woodShort);
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5530:   835	- wood burn = `1e18` wood per base per tick, plus `0.5e18` wood per living clansman per tick
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5559:Not clean. Three requested fixes verify cleanly: wood formula for a non-starving 4-clansman clan is `3e18`, winter events are back to `uint64`, existing wheat plots lock with `remainingWheat = 0` and `regrowUntilTick = 0`, and spec §7.3 is patched.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5595:If starvation kills a clansman, wood burn is calculated afterward from the reduced `livingClansmen`. A 4-clansman clan that loses one to starvation that tick burns `2.5e18`, not `3e18`. The requested non-starving case passes, but the same-tick ordering is a fairness wrinkle.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5611:- Spec §7.3 now documents per-base plus per-clansman wood burn at `docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md@1210e90:831-835`.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5623:Not clean. Three requested fixes verify cleanly: wood formula for a non-starving 4-clansman clan is `3e18`, winter events are back to `uint64`, existing wheat plots lock with `remainingWheat = 0` and `regrowUntilTick = 0`, and spec §7.3 is patched.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5659:If starvation kills a clansman, wood burn is calculated afterward from the reduced `livingClansmen`. A 4-clansman clan that loses one to starvation that tick burns `2.5e18`, not `3e18`. The requested non-starving case passes, but the same-tick ordering is a fairness wrinkle.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5675:- Spec §7.3 now documents per-base plus per-clansman wood burn at `docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md@1210e90:831-835`.
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-opus-4-6.md:7:**CLEAN (conditional)** — no HIGH findings. The reservation-based building system (reserve-at-queue, debit-at-settle) is correctly designed. Death-refund, cancellation, and cross-clan isolation all work. The R2 fixes properly address all 6 R1 HIGHs without introducing regressions. Two MEDIUM findings: (1) cross-building-type order switching can cause false validation rejections because the about-to-be-freed reservation from the OLD building type isn't accounted for in the NEW type's validation, and (2) the `_min(held.cost, currentCost)` settlement pattern combined with non-monotonic cost tables allows a small resource underpayment (~5 wood) when reservations shift levels due to cancellation. Both are correctness deviations with negligible gameplay impact. **Recommend merge** — both MEDIUMs are follow-up-issue material, not blockers.
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-opus-4-6.md:19:Each validation function only considers the SAME-TYPE existing reservation when computing `releasedWood`/etc. If clansman A has an active **wall** upgrade reservation holding 20 wood, and you submit a **base** upgrade order for the same clansman, the base validation sees `_reservedWoodByClan` still includes the wall's 20 wood (because refund happens AFTER validation in `_validateAndInstallMission`). The `releasedWood` in `_validateUpgradeBaseOrder` only checks `_baseUpgradeReservations[clansmanId]`, which is empty.
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-opus-4-6.md:21:**Concrete scenario:** Clan has 50 wood. Clansman A has wall reservation (20 wood reserved). Available for base validation = 50 - 20 = 30. Base 1→2 costs 40 wood → **rejected**. But wall refund would free 20 wood → actual available = 50 → should succeed.
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-opus-4-6.md:33:Wall cost table is non-monotonic: level 1→2 costs 35 wood, level 2→3 costs 30 wood. When reservations shift levels due to cancellation, `_min(held.cost, currentCost)` can debit less than the actual upgrade cost.
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-opus-4-6.md:36:1. Wall=0. A reserves 0→1 (20 wood). B reserves 1→2 (35 wood). C reserves 2→3 (30 wood, 5 iron).
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-opus-4-6.md:38:3. C settles. Actual wall=0. Cost for 0→1 = (20, 0). `_min(30, 20) = 20` wood, `_min(5, 0) = 0` iron. Pays (20, 0). ✓
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-opus-4-6.md:39:4. But if only A cancels: C settles at wall=1. Cost for 1→2 = (35, 0). `_min(30, 35) = 30` wood, `_min(5, 0) = 0` iron. Pays (30, 0) for a 35-wood upgrade. **5 wood underpaid.**
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-opus-4-6.md:41:**Impact:** Negligible economic leak (bounded by max adjacent-level cost delta, ~5-10 wood). Requires 3+ concurrent reservations with cancellation and non-monotonic cost progression. Not economically rational to exploit given the clansman opportunity cost.
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-opus-4-6.md:67:**Event ABI change:** `ResourcesDeposited` tick parameter narrowed from `uint64` to `uint32`. This is a breaking change for any existing indexers. Acceptable for a pre-production contract but worth noting for indexer teams.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1302:+    function test_depositResources_woodCarryMovesToVaultAndClears() public {
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1305:+        uint256 woodDelta = 5e18;
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1307:+        harness.setCarry(csId, woodDelta, 0, 0, 0);
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1323:+        assertEq(harness.getClan(clanId).vaultWood, vaultBefore + woodDelta, "vault wood receives carried wood");
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1324:+        assertEq(harness.getClansman(csId).carryWood, 0, "carry wood is cleared");
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1338:+        bytes32 depositedTopic = keccak256("ResourcesDeposited(uint32,uint32,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint32)");
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1341:+            assertTrue(logs[i].topics[0] != depositedTopic, "empty deposit emits no ResourcesDeposited event");
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1350:+        uint256 woodDelta = 4e18;
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1354:+        harness.setCarry(csId, woodDelta, ironDelta, 0, fishDelta);
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1366:+        assertEq(afterClan.vaultWood, beforeClan.vaultWood + woodDelta, "wood transferred");
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1369:+        assertEq(afterCs.carryWood, 0, "wood carry cleared");
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1397:+        emit IClanWorldEvents.ResourcesDeposited(clanId, csId, 5e18, 2e18, 1e18, 3e18, 1);

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5686:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6825:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2787:docs/reviews/pr200-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7167:  `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol` `ResourcesDeposited`; `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol` `_resolveAction` / `_doDeposit`; ABI JSON.  
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6854:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3684:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.

That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7763:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7621:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1716:+        (uint256 woodCost, uint256 ironCost, uint256 wheatCost, uint256 blueprintCost) = world.getMonumentUpgradeCost(0);
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7843:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8186:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3684:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11228:The spec cross-check confirmed the wood-economy constants in code still differ from the authoritative v4 docs. I’m grabbing the exact spec lines now so the final review can call that out cleanly instead of hand-waving it.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11239:   417	- base yield = `2e18` wood per action tick
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11241:   419	- critical bonus = `+1e18` wood
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11421:- The PR also silently retunes wood gathering/carry economics outside the Phase 8 scope. It changes the cap/yield/crit constants in [IClanWorld.sol:43](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:43) through [IClanWorld.sol:53](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:53), and `_gatherWood` now uses those new values in [ClanWorld.sol:541](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:541) through [ClanWorld.sol:545](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:545). The current v4 docs still specify `WOOD_CAP = 15e18`, `WOOD_BASE_YIELD = 2e18`, `WOOD_CRIT_BONUS = 1e18`, and `WOOD_CRIT_BPS = 2000` in [clanworld_v4_spec.md:417](/home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/01%20Blockchain%20Game%20Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:417) through [clanworld_v4_spec.md:420](/home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/01%20Blockchain%20Game%20Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:420) and [clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:255](/home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/01%20Blockchain%20Game%20Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:255) through [clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:262](/home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/01%20Blockchain%20Game%20Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:262). If this retune is intentional, the spec needs to be updated; if not, this PR is shipping an unrelated economy change.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11452:- This PR also silently changes wood-economy constants outside the building/ranking scope: cap/yield/crit moved in [IClanWorld.sol:43](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:43) through [IClanWorld.sol:53](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:53), while the current spec still says `WOOD_CAP = 15e18`, `WOOD_BASE_YIELD = 2e18`, `WOOD_CRIT_BONUS = 1e18`, `WOOD_CRIT_BPS = 2000` in [clanworld_v4_spec.md:417](</home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:417>) and [clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:255](</home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:255>).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11486:- This PR also silently changes wood-economy constants outside the building/ranking scope: cap/yield/crit moved in [IClanWorld.sol:43](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:43) through [IClanWorld.sol:53](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:53), while the current spec still says `WOOD_CAP = 15e18`, `WOOD_BASE_YIELD = 2e18`, `WOOD_CRIT_BONUS = 1e18`, `WOOD_CRIT_BPS = 2000` in [clanworld_v4_spec.md:417](</home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:417>) and [clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:255](</home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:255>).
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:45:When re-assigning a clansman from one building task to another (e.g., from `UpgradeWall` to `UpgradeBase`), `submitClanOrders` will correctly refund the old wall reservation before processing the new base reservation. However, the new order is validated via `_validateUpgradeBaseOrder` *before* the refund occurs. `_validateUpgradeBaseOrder` only considers `_baseUpgradeReservations` for its `releasedWood` calculation, ignoring the wood locked in the active wall reservation. If the vault lacks surplus resources, the switch will falsely revert with `ERR_MISSING_RESOURCES`.
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:52:`_doDeposit` currently aborts early without emitting a `ResourcesDeposited` event if the clansman's carry is empty. This is an intended UI optimization, but it still enforces the 1-tick `DEPOSIT_DURATION_TICKS` penalty. 
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:5:PR #250 is a substantial Phase-10 cohesion ship: winter schedule, 2× food upkeep + per-clansman wood burn, cold damage → wall degradation → RNG clansman death, wheat plot WinterLocked transitions, and a unified `_markClanDead` helper. The structural shape is good — death routes through `_markClansmanDead`/`_markClanDead`, dead clans short-circuit at `submitClanOrders` and `_settleCompletingMissions`, defenders are cleared, vault resources burn, gold survives. Tests are dense and exercise most happy paths.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:7:But three real issues land: (1) **cold-damage RNG uses `_world.currentTickSeed` instead of the historical `_tickSeeds[tick]`**, which the rest of the codebase consistently uses for lazy-settle RNG — this breaks lazy-settle determinism and is timing-exploitable; (2) **starvation kill condition has an inverted-fairness guard** that grants pre-winter-starvers immunity from winter starvation kills; (3) **wood burn semantics deviate from spec** (per-clansman 0.5e18 vs spec's per-base 1e18). Plus a handful of MEDIUM/LOW smaller issues. Recommend NOT merging until at least the first two are addressed (or explicitly resolved as design changes with an ADR/spec patch).
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:54:Spec (§7.3, v4.3 K.1): `WINTER_WOOD_BURN_PER_BASE = 1e18` — fixed per base, regardless of clansman count. PR replaces with `WINTER_WOOD_BURN_PER_CLANSMAN = 5e17` (0.5 wood) and computes `woodNeeded = livingClansmen * 0.5e18`. At full strength (4 clansmen) that's **2e18 per tick = 2× spec**; as clansmen die, burn drops. This may be an intentional balance change (cold deaths cascade less when only one clansman remains), but the spec is the canonical reference and was not updated. Either:
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:179:6. **Spec compliance is solid except for M1 (wood burn).** §A2 winter timing is correctly implemented as a global recurring schedule (PR v4.5 addendum supersedes the per-season-reset older spec). §10 elimination — vault burn yes, gold preservation yes, mission/defender cleanup yes. §10.2/§10.3 cold damage thresholds (every 2) — correct via integer-division-step pattern. §10.4 wheat plot WinterLocked + restart — correct, modulo M2 boundary.

docs/reviews/pr153-review-claude-opus.md:33:| 1 | `contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol` | 1093–1100 | **Stale queue guard incomplete for same-type mission replacement.** `ScheduledMarketAction` has no `missionNonce` field. Guard only checks `m.action != sma.action`. Replacing MarketSell(wood,100) with MarketSell(iron,50) for the same tick leaves the old queue entry valid — heartbeat executes both with stale parameters. **PR #137 Finding #1 — NOT FIXED.** Wave 2 Agent 2.2 traced full lifecycle confirming exploit: old row runs with wrong asset/size, corrupting vault/pool state. No test covers this scenario. | Correctness |
docs/reviews/pr153-review-claude-opus.md:41:| 4 | `contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol` | 1408–1413 | **`PoolsSeeded` event argument order mismatches `IClanWorld`.** Interface declares `(wood, wheat, fish, iron)` but implementation emits `(wood, iron, wheat, fish)`. Indexers decoding by position assign wrong pool addresses. PR #137 #2 — NOT FIXED. | Correctness |
docs/reviews/pr153-review-claude-opus.md:198:- #4: Fix `PoolsSeeded` emit order to match IClanWorld: `(wood, wheat, fish, iron)`

docs/reviews/pr193-codereview-codex-5-4.md:304:+        bytes32 depositedTopic = keccak256("ResourcesDeposited(uint32,uint32,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint32)");
docs/reviews/pr193-codereview-codex-5-4.md:307:+            assertTrue(logs[i].topics[0] != depositedTopic, "empty deposit emits no ResourcesDeposited event");

docs/reviews/pr193-codereview-codex-5-4.md:320:+        harness.setCarry(csId, woodDelta, ironDelta, 0, fishDelta);

docs/reviews/pr193-codereview-codex-5-4.md:335:+        assertEq(afterCs.carryWood, 0, "wood carry cleared");
docs/reviews/pr193-codereview-codex-5-4.md:363:+        emit IClanWorldEvents.ResourcesDeposited(clanId, csId, 5e18, 2e18, 1e18, 3e18, 1);

docs/reviews/pr199-r3-synthesis.md:20:| **H2** | `packages/contracts/abi/IClanWorld.json` (full file) + `packages/runner/src/runnerCastHeartbeat.ts:22, :164` + `packages/shared/src/adapters/IChainClient.ts:24, :280, :377` | 5.4 + 5.5 + 4.7 + cloud Copilot = **4/5 + cloud** | **HIGH** | **ABI artifact + hardcoded TS adapters all stale.** Verified: `WallUpgraded`, `BaseUpgraded`, `MonumentUpgraded` events declared in `IClanWorld.sol:548-550` + emitted in `ClanWorld.sol:822/869/906` — completely absent from committed ABI JSON. `ResourcesDeposited` still encodes old `wood/iron/wheat/fish/atTick uint64`; source now uses `woodDelta/.../tick uint32`. Topic-0 = `keccak(canonicalSig)` — uint64→uint32 rotates the topic hash, indexers silently miss every deposit event. Plus: `WorldSnapshot` + `Mission` tuple shifts (`currentSeasonNumber`, `nextHeartbeatAtTick`, `submittedAtTick`, `executesAtTick`, `settlesAtTick`) inserted before existing fields → handwritten viem ABIs in `IChainClient` + `runnerCastHeartbeat` decode wrong slots → `getCurrentTick()` + `readNextHeartbeatAt()` broken. **Fix:** regenerate ABI JSON via `forge build` + copy. Update hardcoded TS structs OR import canonical ABI fragment. Add CI/pretest guard that diffs regenerated against committed + fails on drift. |
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-synthesis.md:29:| H6 | `ClanWorld.sol:448, :1956, :2157, :2221, :2294` | Codex 5.4 = 1/5 (CRITICAL) | HIGH | **`_reserved*ByClan` only affects order validation — `_applyUpkeep()` and `_deductFromVault()` still spend raw vault.** Queued upgrade does NOT lock reserved resources against upkeep/market sells. Same TOCTOU class the reservation fix was meant to remove: upgrade validates successfully → upkeep/market consumes the "held" wheat/wood/iron → settlement fails with `ERR_MISSING_RESOURCES`. Even though reservation accounting stays consistent, the lock is incomplete. **Fix:** escrow/debit reserved resources at queue time, OR make every vault consumer spend only from `vault - reserved`. |
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-synthesis.md:37:| M1 | 5.4 + 5.5 + 4.7 + 4.6 + Gemini = **5/5 consensus** | **Cross-type upgrade-mission switch falsely reverts when resources are tight.** Validation runs before refund of previous building's reservation. Switching from UpgradeWall → UpgradeBase: validation only releases same-type reservation, sees old wall's wood still in `_reservedWoodByClan` → `ERR_MISSING_RESOURCES`. UX-only no data corruption. |
docs/reviews/pr199-synthesis.md:34:| M2 | 5.4 + 5.5 | **PR silently retunes wood economy constants OUTSIDE Phase 8 scope** — `WOOD_CAP`/`WOOD_BASE_YIELD`/`WOOD_CRIT_BONUS`/`WOOD_CRIT_BPS` changed in `IClanWorld.sol:43-53`. Spec still has original values. Either patch spec OR revert PR diff. |
docs/reviews/pr199-synthesis.md:53:- 2/3 overlap: H2 (durations off-spec) + H4 (BuildWall race) + M2 (wood economy retune)
docs/reviews/pr199-synthesis.md:66:7. **M2:** revert wood economy constants OR patch spec
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:19:Phase 10 ships: winter schedule (10.1) + winter upkeep 2x food + wood burn (10.2) + cold damage walls degrade then RNG clansman death (10.3) + crop transitions WinterLocked (10.4) + clan death (10.5).
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:323:+          "name": "woodShort",
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:503:+    /// @dev Apply one tick of upkeep. Marks starvation if insufficient food and cold damage if winter wood is short.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:533:+            uint256 woodNeeded = uint256(clan.livingClansmen) * ClanWorldConstants.WINTER_WOOD_BURN_PER_CLANSMAN;
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:534:+            if (clan.vaultWood >= woodNeeded) {
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:535:+                clan.vaultWood -= woodNeeded;
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:537:+                uint256 woodShort = woodNeeded - clan.vaultWood;
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:543:+                emit ClanColdShortage(clan.clanId, _eventTick(tick), woodShort);

docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-synthesis.md:19:| H3: `_applyUpkeep` wood-burn semantics | 5.5 + 4.7 = **2/3** | HIGH | **Wood burn implemented as `livingClansmen × 0.5e18` (per-clansman) but spec §7.3 says `1e18` per BASE per tick.** A 4-clansman clan burns 2e18 vs spec's 1e18. New tests codify the wrong expectation. **Fix:** flat `1e18` per base per winter tick, regardless of clansman count. Update tests. |
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-synthesis.md:34:- **Codex 5.5:** NEEDS FIXES — 4 HIGH (RNG + wood burn + starvation guard + ABI break) + MED
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-synthesis.md:35:- **Opus 4.7:** NEEDS FIXES — 3 HIGH (RNG + starvation guard + wood burn) + several MED
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-synthesis.md:40:- 2/3 overlap: 1 HIGH (wood burn) + 1 MED (wheat plot clear)

docs/reviews/pr200-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:2036:+    function _hasVaultResources(Clan storage clan, uint256 wood, uint256 wheat, uint256 fish, uint256 iron)

docs/reviews/pr200-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8694:One focused pass is back with an event-surface regression on `ResourcesDeposited`. I’m still waiting on the OTC and boundary-token explorers while I tighten the remaining local findings around stale-clan settlement and ABI/event mismatches, since those are the seams most likely to bite after the fix-round.
docs/reviews/pr200-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8723:  `ResourcesDeposited` changed signature from the previous `...uint32 tick` shape to `...uint64 atTick`, which changes the event topic hash. The PR updates local tests, but any existing indexer/UI/automation filtering the old topic will silently stop seeing deposit events after upgrade. Suggested fix: preserve the old event signature, version the event name, or ship explicit migration coverage for downstream consumers.
docs/reviews/pr200-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8759:  `ResourcesDeposited` changed signature from the previous `...uint32 tick` shape to `...uint64 atTick`, which changes the event topic hash. The PR updates local tests, but any existing indexer/UI/automation filtering the old topic will silently stop seeing deposit events after upgrade. Suggested fix: preserve the old event signature, version the event name, or ship explicit migration coverage for downstream consumers.

docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:77:+- wood burn = `1e18` wood per base per tick, plus `0.5e18` wood per living clansman per tick
