docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7286:4696:400463e:packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1904:            if (_treasury.woodToken == address(0)) return StatusCode.ERR_MARKET_UNSUPPORTED_TOKEN;
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7481:5435:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-synthesis.md:29:| M1 | `ClanWorld.sol:440-447` | **Wood burn uses post-starvation living count on same tick.** If starvation kills a clansman, wood burn is calculated AFTER from the reduced `livingClansmen`. A 4-clansman clan that loses one to starvation on a tick burns `2.5e18`, not `3e18` (per-clansman 0.5×3 + per-base 1.0). Fairness wrinkle — same-tick ordering. Liam: deliberate or fix? |
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7483:5437:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-synthesis.md:38:- **Codex 5.5:** NEEDS_FIXES — 2 HIGH (starvation edge + ABI staleness) + 2 MED (wood burn ordering + winter-mint plot state).
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7651:6375:docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:7:But three real issues land: (1) **cold-damage RNG uses `_world.currentTickSeed` instead of the historical `_tickSeeds[tick]`**, which the rest of the codebase consistently uses for lazy-settle RNG — this breaks lazy-settle determinism and is timing-exploitable; (2) **starvation kill condition has an inverted-fairness guard** that grants pre-winter-starvers immunity from winter starvation kills; (3) **wood burn semantics deviate from spec** (per-clansman 0.5e18 vs spec's per-base 1e18). Plus a handful of MEDIUM/LOW smaller issues. Recommend NOT merging until at least the first two are addressed (or explicitly resolved as design changes with an ADR/spec patch).
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8035:9328:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-opus-4-7.md:7:The four targeted fixes (H2 starvation, H3 wood burn, H4 uint64 events, H5 wheat plot clear) are correctly implemented and exercised by 15 new tests, all passing. Spec §7.3 was patched as required. **However, the fix-round introduces a new HIGH-severity correctness bug**: the heartbeat-eager `_resetColdDamageForAllClans()` call at winter-end destroys the mid-winter `coldDamage` value for any clan whose `lastSettledTick` is inside the winter window, causing the lazy re-settle to under-count cold consequences (wall degradations / clansman deaths). I reproduced it locally — a clan settled mid-winter with `coldDamage=3` ends winter with `wallLevel=6` instead of `5` for the otherwise-identical never-settled clan. The fix is a one-liner: drop the eager reset; the in-loop / post-loop checks in `_settleClan` already handle the boundary correctly.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8045:9434:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:233:- **codex-5-4 produced no signal** — the session was killed mid-investigation; treat its file as empty. codex-5-5 is the only complete prior automated review and identified H2/H3/H4 (originally numbered H1/H2/H3 in their report) plus several MEDIUMs that overlap with M3/M5 here. This review adds H1 (cold/starvation refund miss — current bug, not theoretical), M1 (resource-burn ordering at settle), M2 (silent failure path), M6 (wood scope creep), M7 (event ABI break), L1 (`_` prefix), L2 (error-code regression), L7 (test gaps).
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8073:10269:| M1 | `ClanWorld.sol:440-447` | **Wood burn uses post-starvation living count on same tick.** If starvation kills a clansman, wood burn is calculated AFTER from the reduced `livingClansmen`. A 4-clansman clan that loses one to starvation on a tick burns `2.5e18`, not `3e18` (per-clansman 0.5×3 + per-base 1.0). Fairness wrinkle — same-tick ordering. Liam: deliberate or fix? |
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8076:10278:- **Codex 5.5:** NEEDS_FIXES — 2 HIGH (starvation edge + ABI staleness) + 2 MED (wood burn ordering + winter-mint plot state).
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8312:`heartbeat()` calls `_settleCompletingMissions(closedTick)` directly, but that path only resolves missions. It skips the canonical `_settleClan` per-tick ordering: upkeep, winter food/wood burn, cold/starvation deaths, crop regrow, and `lastSettledTick` advancement. A clan can have a due deposit/market/harvest mission completed by heartbeat, then later lazy settlement replays earlier winter upkeep against the already-mutated vault/mission state. Worse, a clansman that should have died from starvation/cold before the due tick can still complete the mission.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8314:Suggested fix: refactor heartbeat mission settlement to use the same per-tick settlement pipeline for touched clans through `closedTick + 1`, with `lastSettledTick` updated, before market execution. Add an integration test where a mission settles during winter while the clan has insufficient food/wood.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8369:`heartbeat()` calls `_settleCompletingMissions(closedTick)` directly, but that path only resolves missions. It skips the canonical `_settleClan` per-tick ordering: upkeep, winter food/wood burn, cold/starvation deaths, crop regrow, and `lastSettledTick` advancement. A clan can have a due deposit/market/harvest mission completed by heartbeat, then later lazy settlement replays earlier winter upkeep against the already-mutated vault/mission state. Worse, a clansman that should have died from starvation/cold before the due tick can still complete the mission.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8371:Suggested fix: refactor heartbeat mission settlement to use the same per-tick settlement pipeline for touched clans through `closedTick + 1`, with `lastSettledTick` updated, before market execution. Add an integration test where a mission settles during winter while the clan has insufficient food/wood.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-synthesis.md:23:| H2/SCOPE | `ClanWorld.sol:668, 1237, 1245, 1556, 1858, 2184`; `IClanWorld.sol:134-149` (ActionType enum) | 5.4 + 5.5 = HIGH; 4.7 = SCOPE | DECISION | **Wheelbarrow mechanic only half-implemented.** Carry→vault deposit works (`DepositResources`), but NO production `vault→carry` withdraw path exists. `transferVaultResource()` still reverts. Practical consequence: starter/deposited resources are TRAPPED in the vault — a clan cannot move stockpiled wood to a worker's carry to take to market, or to load up before a bandit raid. Tests bypass this by using harness `setCarry`. **Codex view:** "the intended wheelbarrow anti-bandit mechanic is not actually implementable" — HIGH because Phase 6's R1→Path C reroute was specifically to enable wheelbarrow semantics. **Opus view:** spec gap not PR bug — would need new `WithdrawResources` action type, out of scope for Phase 6. **Liam decision: was Phase 6's Path C scoped to deliver wheelbarrow end-to-end (i.e. add `WithdrawResources` action), or just to fix the vault-vs-carry execution path? If end-to-end → fix-round adds `WithdrawResources`. If execution-only → file as Phase 6B + ship.** |
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:19:Phase 10 ships: winter schedule (10.1) + winter upkeep 2x food + wood burn (10.2) + cold damage walls degrade then RNG clansman death (10.3) + crop transitions WinterLocked (10.4) + clan death (10.5).
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:323:+          "name": "woodShort",
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:503:+    /// @dev Apply one tick of upkeep. Marks starvation if insufficient food and cold damage if winter wood is short.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:533:+            uint256 woodNeeded = uint256(clan.livingClansmen) * ClanWorldConstants.WINTER_WOOD_BURN_PER_CLANSMAN;
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:534:+            if (clan.vaultWood >= woodNeeded) {
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:535:+                clan.vaultWood -= woodNeeded;
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:537:+                uint256 woodShort = woodNeeded - clan.vaultWood;
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:543:+                emit ClanColdShortage(clan.clanId, _eventTick(tick), woodShort);
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:950:         _treasury.woodToken = tokens[0];
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1121:+    event ClanColdShortage(uint32 indexed clanId, uint32 tick, uint256 woodShort);
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1475:+        assertEq(clan.vaultWood, 98e18, "winter wood burn should be per clansman");
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1476:+        assertEq(clan.coldDamage, 0, "sufficient wood should avoid cold damage");
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1477:+        assertEq(clan.wallLevel, 2, "sufficient wood should not degrade wall");
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1478:+        assertEq(clan.livingClansmen, 4, "sufficient wood should not kill clansmen");
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1495:+        assertEq(clan.vaultWood, 0, "short winter burn should consume remaining wood");
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1566:+        assertEq(clan.vaultWood, 0, "wood should burn on death");
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1592:+        assertEq(clan.vaultWood, 0, "wood should burn on death");
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1631:+        assertEq(clanAAfter.vaultWood, clanABefore.vaultWood, "clan A wood should not burn");
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1651:+        assertEq(clan.vaultWood, 100e18, "post-winter should not burn wood");

docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7442:    /// @dev Apply one tick of upkeep. Marks starvation if insufficient food and cold damage if winter wood is short.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7486:    /// @dev Compute loot value per v4 spec §6.9: wood=1, wheat=1, fish=2, iron=4 points.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7582:            wood: _poolReserves(_treasury.woodToken, _treasury.woodGoldPool),
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7669:NEEDS FIXES. The A2 winter cadence change to first winter at tick `110` is correct, but the phase has several merge blockers: winter upkeep is overcharging wood, cold-death RNG is not deterministic under lazy settlement, starvation now has an extra lethal path that conflicts with the written starvation rules, and winter event ABI compatibility is broken.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7675:`packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol::_applyUpkeep` — Winter wood burn is implemented as per-clansman (`livingClansmen * 0.5e18`), but spec §7.3 says `1e18` wood per base per tick. A normal 4-clansman clan burns `2e18`; the new test codifies the wrong expectation.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7708:NEEDS FIXES. The A2 winter cadence change to first winter at tick `110` is correct, but the phase has several merge blockers: winter upkeep is overcharging wood, cold-death RNG is not deterministic under lazy settlement, starvation now has an extra lethal path that conflicts with the written starvation rules, and winter event ABI compatibility is broken.
docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7714:`packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol::_applyUpkeep` — Winter wood burn is implemented as per-clansman (`livingClansmen * 0.5e18`), but spec §7.3 says `1e18` wood per base per tick. A normal 4-clansman clan burns `2e18`; the new test codifies the wrong expectation.

docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3284:+    function setBanditCarry(uint32 banditId, uint256 wood, uint256 wheat, uint256 fish, uint256 iron, uint256 gold)
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3287:+        _bandits[banditId].carryWood = wood;

docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:367:+          "name": "woodShort",
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:524:+    /// @dev Apply one tick of upkeep. Marks starvation if insufficient food and cold damage if winter wood is short.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:559:+            uint256 woodNeeded = ClanWorldConstants.WINTER_WOOD_BURN_PER_BASE + uint256(livingBeforeStarvation)
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:561:+            if (clan.vaultWood >= woodNeeded) {
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:562:+                clan.vaultWood -= woodNeeded;
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:564:+                uint256 woodShort = woodNeeded - clan.vaultWood;
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:570:+                emit ClanColdShortage(clan.clanId, tick, woodShort);
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:981:         _treasury.woodToken = tokens[0];

docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1501:+        assertEq(clan.coldDamage, 0, "sufficient wood should avoid cold damage");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1502:+        assertEq(clan.wallLevel, 2, "sufficient wood should not degrade wall");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1503:+        assertEq(clan.livingClansmen, 4, "sufficient wood should not kill clansmen");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1520:+        assertEq(clan.vaultWood, 0, "short winter burn should consume remaining wood");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1608:+        assertEq(clan.vaultWood, 0, "wood should be consumed after shortage");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1609:+        assertEq(clan.coldDamage, 1, "wood burn should use the pre-starvation living count");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1709:+        assertEq(clan.vaultWood, 0, "wood should burn on death");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1735:+        assertEq(clan.vaultWood, 0, "wood should burn on death");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1774:+        assertEq(clanAAfter.vaultWood, clanABefore.vaultWood, "clan A wood should not burn");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1794:+        assertEq(clan.vaultWood, 100e18, "post-winter should not burn wood");

docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5435:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-synthesis.md:29:| M1 | `ClanWorld.sol:440-447` | **Wood burn uses post-starvation living count on same tick.** If starvation kills a clansman, wood burn is calculated AFTER from the reduced `livingClansmen`. A 4-clansman clan that loses one to starvation on a tick burns `2.5e18`, not `3e18` (per-clansman 0.5×3 + per-base 1.0). Fairness wrinkle — same-tick ordering. Liam: deliberate or fix? |
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5437:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-synthesis.md:38:- **Codex 5.5:** NEEDS_FIXES — 2 HIGH (starvation edge + ABI staleness) + 2 MED (wood burn ordering + winter-mint plot state).

docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5702:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2425:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:403:- winter wood burn
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5710:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2436:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:855:If a base cannot pay the required winter wood burn for a tick:
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5862:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2982:docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:5:PR #250 is a substantial Phase-10 cohesion ship: winter schedule, 2× food upkeep + per-clansman wood burn, cold damage → wall degradation → RNG clansman death, wheat plot WinterLocked transitions, and a unified `_markClanDead` helper. The structural shape is good — death routes through `_markClansmanDead`/`_markClanDead`, dead clans short-circuit at `submitClanOrders` and `_settleCompletingMissions`, defenders are cleared, vault resources burn, gold survives. Tests are dense and exercise most happy paths.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5866:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2987:docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:179:6. **Spec compliance is solid except for M1 (wood burn).** §A2 winter timing is correctly implemented as a global recurring schedule (PR v4.5 addendum supersedes the per-season-reset older spec). §10 elimination — vault burn yes, gold preservation yes, mission/defender cleanup yes. §10.2/§10.3 cold damage thresholds (every 2) — correct via integer-division-step pattern. §10.4 wheat plot WinterLocked + restart — correct, modulo M2 boundary.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5867:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3041:docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:19:Phase 10 ships: winter schedule (10.1) + winter upkeep 2x food + wood burn (10.2) + cold damage walls degrade then RNG clansman death (10.3) + crop transitions WinterLocked (10.4) + clan death (10.5).

docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6020:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3481:docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6919:Phase 10 ships: winter schedule (10.1) + winter upkeep 2x food + wood burn (10.2) + cold damage walls degrade then RNG clansman death (10.3) + crop transitions WinterLocked (10.4) + clan death (10.5).
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6023:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3504:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5052:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:19:Phase 10 ships: winter schedule (10.1) + winter upkeep 2x food + wood burn (10.2) + cold damage walls degrade then RNG clansman death (10.3) + crop transitions WinterLocked (10.4) + clan death (10.5).
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6024:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3505:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5917:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:19:Phase 10 ships: winter schedule (10.1) + winter upkeep 2x food + wood burn (10.2) + cold damage walls degrade then RNG clansman death (10.3) + crop transitions WinterLocked (10.4) + clan death (10.5).
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6029:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3946:docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9028:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:5:PR #250 is a substantial Phase-10 cohesion ship: winter schedule, 2× food upkeep + per-clansman wood burn, cold damage → wall degradation → RNG clansman death, wheat plot WinterLocked transitions, and a unified `_markClanDead` helper. The structural shape is good — death routes through `_markClansmanDead`/`_markClanDead`, dead clans short-circuit at `submitClanOrders` and `_settleCompletingMissions`, defenders are cleared, vault resources burn, gold survives. Tests are dense and exercise most happy paths.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6030:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3947:docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9032:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:179:6. **Spec compliance is solid except for M1 (wood burn).** §A2 winter timing is correctly implemented as a global recurring schedule (PR v4.5 addendum supersedes the per-season-reset older spec). §10 elimination — vault burn yes, gold preservation yes, mission/defender cleanup yes. §10.2/§10.3 cold damage thresholds (every 2) — correct via integer-division-step pattern. §10.4 wheat plot WinterLocked + restart — correct, modulo M2 boundary.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6058:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4333:If a base cannot pay the required winter wood burn for a tick:
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6079:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5012:The requested fixes mostly line up in the contract diff: starvation now uses `< tick`, wood burn is base plus per-clansman, winter events are `uint64`, wheat lock clears existing plots, and §7.3 is patched. The more interesting review risk is in the edges around newly derived ABI/view shapes and winter-time minting.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6082:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5130:1210e90 fix(contracts): Phase 10 super-swarm r2 — starvation guard/timing + wood burn per-base + uint64 ABI revert + wheat plot clear (Closes #283-companion) (#287)
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6110:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5559:Not clean. Three requested fixes verify cleanly: wood formula for a non-starving 4-clansman clan is `3e18`, winter events are back to `uint64`, existing wheat plots lock with `remainingWheat = 0` and `regrowUntilTick = 0`, and spec §7.3 is patched.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6118:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5595:If starvation kills a clansman, wood burn is calculated afterward from the reduced `livingClansmen`. A 4-clansman clan that loses one to starvation that tick burns `2.5e18`, not `3e18`. The requested non-starving case passes, but the same-tick ordering is a fairness wrinkle.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6122:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5623:Not clean. Three requested fixes verify cleanly: wood formula for a non-starving 4-clansman clan is `3e18`, winter events are back to `uint64`, existing wheat plots lock with `remainingWheat = 0` and `regrowUntilTick = 0`, and spec §7.3 is patched.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6130:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5659:If starvation kills a clansman, wood burn is calculated afterward from the reduced `livingClansmen`. A 4-clansman clan that loses one to starvation that tick burns `2.5e18`, not `3e18`. The requested non-starving case passes, but the same-tick ordering is a fairness wrinkle.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6146:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5052:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:19:Phase 10 ships: winter schedule (10.1) + winter upkeep 2x food + wood burn (10.2) + cold damage walls degrade then RNG clansman death (10.3) + crop transitions WinterLocked (10.4) + clan death (10.5).
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6151:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5316:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_spec.md:855:If a base cannot pay the required winter wood burn for a tick:
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6154:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5383:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:818:Because upkeep is applied before deposit effects, a worker arriving home with wood/food in carry does **not** save the base from that same tick’s winter burn or upkeep shortage.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6158:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5470:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:96:then the base **still takes the winter wood-burn failure / cold-damage consequence for that tick**.

docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6185:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7239:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:96:then the base **still takes the winter wood-burn failure / cold-damage consequence for that tick**.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6195:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7338:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:855:If a base cannot pay the required winter wood burn for a tick:
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6198:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7402:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:818:Because upkeep is applied before deposit effects, a worker arriving home with wood/food in carry does **not** save the base from that same tick’s winter burn or upkeep shortage.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6374:docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:5:PR #250 is a substantial Phase-10 cohesion ship: winter schedule, 2× food upkeep + per-clansman wood burn, cold damage → wall degradation → RNG clansman death, wheat plot WinterLocked transitions, and a unified `_markClanDead` helper. The structural shape is good — death routes through `_markClansmanDead`/`_markClanDead`, dead clans short-circuit at `submitClanOrders` and `_settleCompletingMissions`, defenders are cleared, vault resources burn, gold survives. Tests are dense and exercise most happy paths.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6375:docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:7:But three real issues land: (1) **cold-damage RNG uses `_world.currentTickSeed` instead of the historical `_tickSeeds[tick]`**, which the rest of the codebase consistently uses for lazy-settle RNG — this breaks lazy-settle determinism and is timing-exploitable; (2) **starvation kill condition has an inverted-fairness guard** that grants pre-winter-starvers immunity from winter starvation kills; (3) **wood burn semantics deviate from spec** (per-clansman 0.5e18 vs spec's per-base 1e18). Plus a handful of MEDIUM/LOW smaller issues. Recommend NOT merging until at least the first two are addressed (or explicitly resolved as design changes with an ADR/spec patch).
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6399:docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-opus-4-7.md:179:6. **Spec compliance is solid except for M1 (wood burn).** §A2 winter timing is correctly implemented as a global recurring schedule (PR v4.5 addendum supersedes the per-season-reset older spec). §10 elimination — vault burn yes, gold preservation yes, mission/defender cleanup yes. §10.2/§10.3 cold damage thresholds (every 2) — correct via integer-division-step pattern. §10.4 wheat plot WinterLocked + restart — correct, modulo M2 boundary.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6414:docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:524:+    /// @dev Apply one tick of upkeep. Marks starvation if insufficient food and cold damage if winter wood is short.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6544:docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1500:+        assertEq(clan.vaultWood, 97e18, "winter wood burn should include base and per clansman");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6545:docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1501:+        assertEq(clan.coldDamage, 0, "sufficient wood should avoid cold damage");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6551:docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1520:+        assertEq(clan.vaultWood, 0, "short winter burn should consume remaining wood");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6580:docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1609:+        assertEq(clan.coldDamage, 1, "wood burn should use the pre-starvation living count");
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6627:docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1794:+        assertEq(clan.vaultWood, 100e18, "post-winter should not burn wood");

docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8912:docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7442:    /// @dev Apply one tick of upkeep. Marks starvation if insufficient food and cold damage if winter wood is short.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8921:docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7669:NEEDS FIXES. The A2 winter cadence change to first winter at tick `110` is correct, but the phase has several merge blockers: winter upkeep is overcharging wood, cold-death RNG is not deterministic under lazy settlement, starvation now has an extra lethal path that conflicts with the written starvation rules, and winter event ABI compatibility is broken.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8926:docs/reviews/r2-phase10/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7708:NEEDS FIXES. The A2 winter cadence change to first winter at tick `110` is correct, but the phase has several merge blockers: winter upkeep is overcharging wood, cold-death RNG is not deterministic under lazy settlement, starvation now has an extra lethal path that conflicts with the written starvation rules, and winter event ABI compatibility is broken.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9328:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-opus-4-7.md:7:The four targeted fixes (H2 starvation, H3 wood burn, H4 uint64 events, H5 wheat plot clear) are correctly implemented and exercised by 15 new tests, all passing. Spec §7.3 was patched as required. **However, the fix-round introduces a new HIGH-severity correctness bug**: the heartbeat-eager `_resetColdDamageForAllClans()` call at winter-end destroys the mid-winter `coldDamage` value for any clan whose `lastSettledTick` is inside the winter window, causing the lazy re-settle to under-count cold consequences (wall degradations / clansman deaths). I reproduced it locally — a clan settled mid-winter with `coldDamage=3` ends winter with `wallLevel=6` instead of `5` for the otherwise-identical never-settled clan. The fix is a one-liner: drop the eager reset; the in-loop / post-loop checks in `_settleClan` already handle the boundary correctly.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9336:docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-opus-4-7.md:41:Both clans had identical inputs (4 clansmen, no wood, full food, wallLevel=10, 10 winter ticks of shortage). They diverge by 1 wall-degradation. In the worst case (clansman deaths instead of wall absorption), this is a missed clansman death — directly affecting `livingClansmen` and elimination outcomes.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9434:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:233:- **codex-5-4 produced no signal** — the session was killed mid-investigation; treat its file as empty. codex-5-5 is the only complete prior automated review and identified H2/H3/H4 (originally numbered H1/H2/H3 in their report) plus several MEDIUMs that overlap with M3/M5 here. This review adds H1 (cold/starvation refund miss — current bug, not theoretical), M1 (resource-burn ordering at settle), M2 (silent failure path), M6 (wood scope creep), M7 (event ABI break), L1 (`_` prefix), L2 (error-code regression), L7 (test gaps).

docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9917:- wood burn = `1e18` wood per base per tick
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9937:If a base cannot pay the required winter wood burn for a tick:
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9977:- Level 1: `20 wood`
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9978:- Level 2: `35 wood`
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9979:- Level 3: `30 wood + 5 iron`
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9980:- Level 4: `40 wood + 10 iron`
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9981:- Level 5: `50 wood + 15 iron`
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9991:- Level 2: `40 wood + 20 wheat`
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9992:- Level 3: `60 wood + 5 iron + 30 wheat`
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10072:-- wood burn = `1e18` wood per base per tick
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10073:+- wood burn = `1e18` wood per base per tick, plus `0.5e18` wood per living clansman per tick
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10256:- ✅ Spec §7.3 patched to document per-base + per-clansman wood burn
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10269:| M1 | `ClanWorld.sol:440-447` | **Wood burn uses post-starvation living count on same tick.** If starvation kills a clansman, wood burn is calculated AFTER from the reduced `livingClansmen`. A 4-clansman clan that loses one to starvation on a tick burns `2.5e18`, not `3e18` (per-clansman 0.5×3 + per-base 1.0). Fairness wrinkle — same-tick ordering. Liam: deliberate or fix? |
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10278:- **Codex 5.5:** NEEDS_FIXES — 2 HIGH (starvation edge + ABI staleness) + 2 MED (wood burn ordering + winter-mint plot state).

docs/reviews/pr200-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8412:  `_settleCompletingMissions()` directly calls `_settleMissionForClansman()` for due missions, but it does not run the normal per-tick `_applyUpkeep()` step or advance `clan.lastSettledTick` like `_settleClan()` does at `386-408`. A heartbeat-settled `DepositResources` can credit the vault, then a later `settleClan()` replays earlier ticks from the stale cursor with those newly deposited resources already present, allowing retroactive upkeep payment and incorrect starvation outcomes. It can also make gathering resolve with stale starvation state.
docs/reviews/pr200-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8474:  `_settleCompletingMissions()` directly calls `_settleMissionForClansman()` for due missions, but it does not run the normal per-tick `_applyUpkeep()` step or advance `clan.lastSettledTick` like `_settleClan()` does at `386-408`. A heartbeat-settled `DepositResources` can credit the vault, then a later `settleClan()` replays earlier ticks from the stale cursor with those newly deposited resources already present, allowing retroactive upkeep payment and incorrect starvation outcomes. It can also make gathering resolve with stale starvation state.
docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-opus-4-6.md:65:- **Starvation + cold ordering is correct and well-tested.** `livingBeforeStarvation` capture → starvation kill → DEAD check → wood burn with pre-death count → cold damage consequence. The `test_winter_starvationWoodBurnUsesPreDeathLivingCount` test directly validates this.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-opus-4-7.md:7:The four targeted fixes (H2 starvation, H3 wood burn, H4 uint64 events, H5 wheat plot clear) are correctly implemented and exercised by 15 new tests, all passing. Spec §7.3 was patched as required. **However, the fix-round introduces a new HIGH-severity correctness bug**: the heartbeat-eager `_resetColdDamageForAllClans()` call at winter-end destroys the mid-winter `coldDamage` value for any clan whose `lastSettledTick` is inside the winter window, causing the lazy re-settle to under-count cold consequences (wall degradations / clansman deaths). I reproduced it locally — a clan settled mid-winter with `coldDamage=3` ends winter with `wallLevel=6` instead of `5` for the otherwise-identical never-settled clan. The fix is a one-liner: drop the eager reset; the in-loop / post-loop checks in `_settleClan` already handle the boundary correctly.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-opus-4-7.md:33:                 → 7 short-wood ticks: 0→7 → 3 wall degradations (crossings at 2,4,6)
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-opus-4-7.md:37:                 → 10 short-wood ticks: 0→10 → 5 wall degradations
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-opus-4-7.md:41:Both clans had identical inputs (4 clansmen, no wood, full food, wallLevel=10, 10 winter ticks of shortage). They diverge by 1 wall-degradation. In the worst case (clansman deaths instead of wall absorption), this is a missed clansman death — directly affecting `livingClansmen` and elimination outcomes.
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-opus-4-7.md:67:event ClanColdShortage(uint32 indexed clanId, uint32 tick, uint256 woodShort);
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-codereview-opus-4-7.md:106:`_settleClan` runs `_applyUpkeep` first, then the regrow check, then mission settlement (line 387-390). Effect: a clansman returning with 100 wood at tick X cannot save the clan from cold-death at tick X if the vault is short — the wood is still on the clansman when `_applyUpkeep` runs. Probably intentional (turn-order: burn → settle → end-of-tick), but worth a comment.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:159:### M6 — Out-of-scope wood gathering changes shipped silently in this PR
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:161:The diff modifies wood gathering behavior in ways NOT mentioned in the PR description:
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:167:Old E[yield] over 4-tick mission: `2e18*4 + 0.2*1e18*4 = 8.8e18` (capped 15). New E[yield]: `0.9*4e18 + 0.1*8e18 = 4.4e18` (capped 10). **Half the wood economy.** This is a Phase 5 game-balance change in a Phase 8 PR.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:171:### M7 — `ResourcesDeposited` event ABI break (uint64 atTick → uint32 tick, field renames) [`IClanWorld.sol:536-544`]
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:175:event ResourcesDeposited(uint32 indexed clanId, uint32 indexed clansmanId,
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:176:    uint256 wood, uint256 iron, uint256 wheat, uint256 fish, uint64 atTick);
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:178:event ResourcesDeposited(uint32 indexed clanId, uint32 indexed clansmanId,
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:179:    uint256 woodDelta, uint256 ironDelta, uint256 wheatDelta, uint256 fishDelta, uint32 tick);
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:231:- **Spec compliance on §8 cost tables is line-perfect** — wall L1=20w, L2=35w, L3=30w+5i, L4=40w+10i, L5=50w+15i; base L2=40w+20wh through L5=100w+15i+50wh; monument L1=30w+20wh through L6=150w+20i+80wh, L7-10=200w+25i+100wh+1e18 blueprint. No drift here. Drift is on durations only (M4) and on ResourcesDeposited event shape (M7).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:233:- **codex-5-4 produced no signal** — the session was killed mid-investigation; treat its file as empty. codex-5-5 is the only complete prior automated review and identified H2/H3/H4 (originally numbered H1/H2/H3 in their report) plus several MEDIUMs that overlap with M3/M5 here. This review adds H1 (cold/starvation refund miss — current bug, not theoretical), M1 (resource-burn ordering at settle), M2 (silent failure path), M6 (wood scope creep), M7 (event ABI break), L1 (`_` prefix), L2 (error-code regression), L7 (test gaps).

docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:307:**Setup:** Defender clan vault has 200 wood. Bandit `attackPower` overshoot carries 50 wood.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:309:**Assert:** Defender clan vault decremented by 50 wood. Bandit `carryWood` incremented by 50.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:315:**Setup:** Defender clan vault has 10 wood. Bandit carry capacity exceeds vault.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:317:**Assert:** Bandit takes at most 10 wood (no underflow). Vault reaches 0, not negative.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:360:**Setup:** Clan has `baseTier = 0`. Sufficient wood + stone.

docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:23:Phase 8 release PR (dev-phase-8-buildings → dev). 6 sub-issues already merged + reviewed individually. R1 super-swarm flagged 6 HIGHs (reservation timing, durations off-spec, rank getters stale, BuildWall+UpgradeWall race, death-refund orphan, level-binding mismatch) + wood economy retune outside scope. R2 fix-round (PR #288, just merged) addressed all 6 HIGHs + reverted wood economy. Tests: 118/118 forge passing.

docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1872:+        uint256 woodDelta = 5e18;
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1874:+        harness.setCarry(csId, woodDelta, 0, 0, 0);
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1890:+        assertEq(harness.getClan(clanId).vaultWood, vaultBefore + woodDelta, "vault wood receives carried wood");
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1891:+        assertEq(harness.getClansman(csId).carryWood, 0, "carry wood is cleared");
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1905:+        bytes32 depositedTopic = keccak256("ResourcesDeposited(uint32,uint32,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint32)");
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1908:+            assertTrue(logs[i].topics[0] != depositedTopic, "empty deposit emits no ResourcesDeposited event");
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1917:+        uint256 woodDelta = 4e18;
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1921:+        harness.setCarry(csId, woodDelta, ironDelta, 0, fishDelta);
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1933:+        assertEq(afterClan.vaultWood, beforeClan.vaultWood + woodDelta, "wood transferred");
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1936:+        assertEq(afterCs.carryWood, 0, "wood carry cleared");
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1964:+        emit IClanWorldEvents.ResourcesDeposited(clanId, csId, 5e18, 2e18, 1e18, 3e18, 1);

docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6531:docs/reviews/pr199-synthesis.md:53:- 2/3 overlap: H2 (durations off-spec) + H4 (BuildWall race) + M2 (wood economy retune)
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6534:docs/reviews/pr198-r2-synthesis.md:23:| H2/SCOPE | `ClanWorld.sol:668, 1237, 1245, 1556, 1858, 2184`; `IClanWorld.sol:134-149` (ActionType enum) | 5.4 + 5.5 = HIGH; 4.7 = SCOPE | DECISION | **Wheelbarrow mechanic only half-implemented.** Carry→vault deposit works (`DepositResources`), but NO production `vault→carry` withdraw path exists. `transferVaultResource()` still reverts. Practical consequence: starter/deposited resources are TRAPPED in the vault — a clan cannot move stockpiled wood to a worker's carry to take to market, or to load up before a bandit raid. Tests bypass this by using harness `setCarry`. **Codex view:** "the intended wheelbarrow anti-bandit mechanic is not actually implementable" — HIGH because Phase 6's R1→Path C reroute was specifically to enable wheelbarrow semantics. **Opus view:** spec gap not PR bug — would need new `WithdrawResources` action type, out of scope for Phase 6. **Liam decision: was Phase 6's Path C scoped to deliver wheelbarrow end-to-end (i.e. add `WithdrawResources` action), or just to fix the vault-vs-carry execution path? If end-to-end → fix-round adds `WithdrawResources`. If execution-only → file as Phase 6B + ship.** |
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6536:docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:23:Phase 8 release PR (dev-phase-8-buildings → dev). 6 sub-issues already merged + reviewed individually. R1 super-swarm flagged 6 HIGHs (reservation timing, durations off-spec, rank getters stale, BuildWall+UpgradeWall race, death-refund orphan, level-binding mismatch) + wood economy retune outside scope. R2 fix-round (PR #288, just merged) addressed all 6 HIGHs + reverted wood economy. Tests: 118/118 forge passing.

docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7072:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5553:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7895:   866	        emit ResourcesDeposited(clanId, cs.clansmanId, w, ir, wh, fi, tick);
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7074:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5686:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.

docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14223:  1427	    // Test 16: twoClan_sellBuyCycle — clan A sells wood, clan B buys wood; both succeed
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14233:- [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:668), [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1858), [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2184) — The carry-based rewrite removed vault-backed selling without adding any production `vault -> carry` path. `DepositResources` is only `carry -> vault`, `MarketSell` now always debits `carry`, and `transferVaultResource()` still reverts. In practice that means starter/deposited resources are trapped in the vault and cannot be sold, and the intended wheelbarrow anti-bandit mechanic is not actually implementable.
docs/reviews/pr198-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14259:- [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:668), [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1858), [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2184) — The carry-based rewrite removed vault-backed selling without adding any production `vault -> carry` path. `DepositResources` is only `carry -> vault`, `MarketSell` now always debits `carry`, and `transferVaultResource()` still reverts. In practice that means starter/deposited resources are trapped in the vault and cannot be sold, and the intended wheelbarrow anti-bandit mechanic is not actually implementable.

docs/reviews/pr250-r3-codereview-opus-4-7.md:122:- **Phase goal delivery:** ✅ Winter cadence (110-tick recurring window), 2x food upkeep, wood burn (base + per-living-clansman), cold damage with wall-then-clansman cascade, wheat-plot lock/unlock, winter-block on `HarvestWheat`, mid-winter mint with locked plots, season-spanning winter, clan elimination with vault burn + gold survival. All match the spec changes in `clanworld_v4_spec.md` §7.

docs/reviews/pr193-codereview-codex-5-5.md:268:+    function test_depositResources_woodCarryMovesToVaultAndClears() public {
docs/reviews/pr193-codereview-codex-5-5.md:271:+        uint256 woodDelta = 5e18;

docs/reviews/pr153-review-codex-5-3.md:33:| 3 | `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol`, `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol` | 1408-1412; 646-650 | `PoolsSeeded` emit order is `(wood, iron, wheat, fish)` but interface declares `(wood, wheat, fish, iron)`, causing ABI-level event interpretation errors for off-chain consumers. | MUST FIX |
docs/reviews/pr153-review-codex-5-3.md:47:| 12 | `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol`, `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorldStub.sol` | 1389-1392; 58-61 | Pool ordering conventions differ between `ClanWorld` and `ClanWorldStub` (`wood/iron/wheat/fish` vs `wood/wheat/fish/iron`), creating script/operator misconfiguration risk. | SHOULD FIX |
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-synthesis-update.md:23:| M1 | `ClanWorld.sol:440-447` (wood burn post-starv) | MED | — | — | MED |
docs/reviews/pr250-r2-synthesis-update.md:35:5. **M1 wood burn ordering** — codex 5.5
docs/reviews/pr137-review-claude-opus.md:53:| 2 | `src/ClanWorld.sol` | 1366–1371 | **`PoolsSeeded` event emission order mismatched vs `IClanWorld`.** Interface declares `(wood, wheat, fish, iron)` but implementation emits `(wood, iron, wheat, fish)`. Args 2–4 are swapped. Off-chain indexers decoding by position assign wrong pool addresses. Confirmed by Agents 1, 4, 8. | **SHOULD FIX** |
docs/reviews/pr137-review-claude-opus.md:130:1. **#2 PoolsSeeded event order** — Swap emit args to match `IClanWorld` declaration: `(wood, wheat, fish, iron)`
docs/reviews/pr137-review-claude-opus.md:137:8. **#10 Market validation pre-init** — Reject market orders when `_treasury.woodToken == address(0)`
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:19:Phase 6 ships: ERC20 boundary tokens (wood/wheat/fish/iron) + 4 constant-product pools + immediate market path + scheduled market FIFO queue + market failure semantics + events surface. Recent fix-round (PR #270) addressed cloud reviewer findings on duplicate enums, unused params, max-actions cap, carry overflow handling, and CLANSMAN_CARRY_CAP rename.

docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3420:         OrderResult[] memory r1 = _submitMarketOrder(clanId, csId1, ActionType.MarketSell, address(woodToken), 5e18, 0);
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3426:         // Give cs0 carry wood. Zero vault so market sell only succeeds if step1 ran first.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3434:-        //   Step 2: _executeScheduledMarketActions(t0+2) → sell fires, 5e18 vault wood → gold
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3437:+        //   Step 2: _executeScheduledMarketActions(t0+3) → sell fires, 5e18 vault wood → gold
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3449:-    //   - cs1 sells wood to UT from Forest: executeAtTick = T0+2
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3452:+    //   - cs1 sells wood to UT from Forest: settlesAtTick = T0+3
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3475:-        // cs1: at Forest, sells wood to UT. Forest→UT = 2 ticks. executeAtTick = t0+2.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3476:+        // cs1: at Forest, sells wood to UT. Forest→UT = 2 ticks. settlesAtTick = t0+3.

docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9329:Phase 6 ships: ERC20 boundary tokens (wood/wheat/fish/iron) + 4 constant-product pools + immediate market path + scheduled market FIFO queue + market failure semantics + events surface. Recent fix-round (PR #270) addressed cloud reviewer findings on duplicate enums, unused params, max-actions cap, carry overflow handling, and CLANSMAN_CARRY_CAP rename.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:9556:Phase 6 ships: ERC20 boundary tokens (wood/wheat/fish/iron) + 4 constant-product pools + immediate market path + scheduled market FIFO queue + market failure semantics + events surface. Recent fix-round (PR #270) addressed cloud reviewer findings on duplicate enums, unused params, max-actions cap, carry overflow handling, and CLANSMAN_CARRY_CAP rename.

docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10339:- Market execution tests are wood-heavy. Pool/token setup covers all four resources, but immediate/scheduled buy/sell execution coverage mostly uses `woodAddr`; add at least one buy/sell seam test across iron/wheat/fish.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10374:- Market execution tests are wood-heavy. Pool/token setup covers all four resources, but immediate/scheduled buy/sell execution coverage mostly uses `woodAddr`; add at least one buy/sell seam test across iron/wheat/fish.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:34:Grep across `packages/contracts/src/` for `.mint(` / `.burn(` returns zero call sites in `ClanWorld.sol`. The only mint path that ever runs is `seedTreasury → _mint` (deployer-only, called once at deploy). The `onlyEngine`-gated external `mint`/`burn` are never invoked by the engine — vault accounting stays in storage scalars (`vaultWood += …`, etc.) and ERC20 transfers happen only at pool seeding (`_transferSeed` → `transferFrom`). If the prize narrative or indexer expects on-chain ERC20 mint events when wood is gathered or burn events on vault drain, this is a silent gap. If the design is genuinely "ERC20 only at the AMM boundary, scalars elsewhere", delete the dead surface (and the `boundaryConfigured`/`engine` storage that backs it) — keeping it advertises a contract you don't honor.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:42:### M2 — Test coverage is wood-only on the four-resource market
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:44:Per the test-coverage agent: only the wood pool is exercised in `test_immediateMarketSell/Buy_*`, `test_buy_*`, `test_sell_*`, `test_scheduledMarket_fifo`, `test_scheduledMarket_executesAllActionsForClosedTick`, and `test_kInvariantHoldsAfterEachSwap`. Iron, wheat, and fish pools are deployed and seeded in every fixture but never traded. Asymmetric pool seed sizes between resources (e.g. `WOOD_CARRY_CAP = 10e18` vs `WHEAT_CAP = 40e18`) increase the chance a per-resource bug would only surface for wheat/fish/iron. Add at least one happy-path scheduled buy + sell for each.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:65:### L1 — `ResourcesDeposited.tick` is `uint32` (event ABI breaking change with cast)
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:95:- **K invariant is enforced** post-swap (`StubPool.sol:74, 94`) but only spot-tested for the wood pool — extend coverage to all four pools.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:96:- **Treasury seeding integrity:** the `_transferSeed → MinimalERC20.transferFrom` chain plus pool's `balanceOf` check at `seed()` is correct — pool reserves cannot exceed actually-deposited balances.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1302:+    function test_depositResources_woodCarryMovesToVaultAndClears() public {
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1305:+        uint256 woodDelta = 5e18;
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1307:+        harness.setCarry(csId, woodDelta, 0, 0, 0);
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1323:+        assertEq(harness.getClan(clanId).vaultWood, vaultBefore + woodDelta, "vault wood receives carried wood");
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1324:+        assertEq(harness.getClansman(csId).carryWood, 0, "carry wood is cleared");
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1338:+        bytes32 depositedTopic = keccak256("ResourcesDeposited(uint32,uint32,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint32)");
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1341:+            assertTrue(logs[i].topics[0] != depositedTopic, "empty deposit emits no ResourcesDeposited event");
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1350:+        uint256 woodDelta = 4e18;
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1354:+        harness.setCarry(csId, woodDelta, ironDelta, 0, fishDelta);
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1366:+        assertEq(afterClan.vaultWood, beforeClan.vaultWood + woodDelta, "wood transferred");
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1369:+        assertEq(afterCs.carryWood, 0, "wood carry cleared");
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1397:+        emit IClanWorldEvents.ResourcesDeposited(clanId, csId, 5e18, 2e18, 1e18, 3e18, 1);

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3684:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6170:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4591:   683	    ///         tokens order: wood, iron, wheat, fish, gold, blueprint.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6525:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6799:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6698:docs/reviews/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5514:        else if (nextLevel <= 10) { woodCost = 200e18; wheatCost = 100e18; ironCost = 25e18; blueprintCost = 1e18; }

docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:19:Phase 6 ships: ERC20 boundary tokens (wood/wheat/fish/iron) + 4 constant-product pools + immediate market path + scheduled market FIFO queue + market failure semantics + events surface. Recent fix-round (PR #270) addressed cloud reviewer findings on duplicate enums, unused params, max-actions cap, carry overflow handling, and CLANSMAN_CARRY_CAP rename.

docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7392:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:19:Phase 6 ships: ERC20 boundary tokens (wood/wheat/fish/iron) + 4 constant-product pools + immediate market path + scheduled market FIFO queue + market failure semantics + events surface. Recent fix-round (PR #270) addressed cloud reviewer findings on duplicate enums, unused params, max-actions cap, carry overflow handling, and CLANSMAN_CARRY_CAP rename.

docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7545:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:19:Phase 6 ships: ERC20 boundary tokens (wood/wheat/fish/iron) + 4 constant-product pools + immediate market path + scheduled market FIFO queue + market failure semantics + events surface. docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10919:  Integration coverage is heavily wood-centric. The PR adds four resources/pools, but the immediate/scheduled trade seam is only exercised with wood; wheat/fish/iron are only covered by getter/seeding assertions, not by end-to-end buy/sell execution tests.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10951:  Integration coverage is heavily wood-centric. The PR adds four resources/pools, but the immediate/scheduled trade seam is only exercised with wood; wheat/fish/iron are only covered by getter/seeding assertions, not by end-to-end buy/sell execution tests.

docs/reviews/pr200-r2-codereview-codex-5-3.md:7745:`initTreasury()` is intended as one-time init, but the sentinel is `_treasury.woodToken == address(0)`. If first init passes `tokens[0] = address(0)`, contract remains “uninitialized” by guard and can be re-initialized with different addresses later. This breaks the one-time invariant and can silently rewire treasury boundaries.  
docs/reviews/pr200-r2-codereview-codex-5-3.md:7793:`initTreasury()` is intended as one-time init, but the sentinel is `_treasury.woodToken == address(0)`. If first init passes `tokens[0] = address(0)`, contract remains “uninitialized” by guard and can be re-initialized with different addresses later. This breaks the one-time invariant and can silently rewire treasury boundaries.  
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:23:Phase 8 release PR (dev-phase-8-buildings → dev). 6 sub-issues already merged + reviewed individually. R1 super-swarm flagged 6 HIGHs (reservation timing, durations off-spec, rank getters stale, BuildWall+UpgradeWall race, death-refund orphan, level-binding mismatch) + wood economy retune outside scope. R2 fix-round (PR #288, just merged) addressed all 6 HIGHs + reverted wood economy. Tests: 118/118 forge passing.

docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1869:+    function test_depositResources_woodCarryMovesToVaultAndClears() public {
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1872:+        uint256 woodDelta = 5e18;
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1874:+        harness.setCarry(csId, woodDelta, 0, 0, 0);
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1890:+        assertEq(harness.getClan(clanId).vaultWood, vaultBefore + woodDelta, "vault wood receives carried wood");
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1891:+        assertEq(harness.getClansman(csId).carryWood, 0, "carry wood is cleared");
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1905:+        bytes32 depositedTopic = keccak256("ResourcesDeposited(uint32,uint32,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint32)");
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1908:+            assertTrue(logs[i].topics[0] != depositedTopic, "empty deposit emits no ResourcesDeposited event");
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1917:+        uint256 woodDelta = 4e18;
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1921:+        harness.setCarry(csId, woodDelta, ironDelta, 0, fishDelta);
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1933:+        assertEq(afterClan.vaultWood, beforeClan.vaultWood + woodDelta, "wood transferred");
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1936:+        assertEq(afterCs.carryWood, 0, "wood carry cleared");
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1964:+        emit IClanWorldEvents.ResourcesDeposited(clanId, csId, 5e18, 2e18, 1e18, 3e18, 1);

docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6506:./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3684:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6521:./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4945:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1691:+        (uint256 wood, uint256 iron, uint256 wheat, uint256 blueprint) = world.getMonumentUpgradeCost(0);

That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.

That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.

docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6845:./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5553:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7895:   866	        emit ResourcesDeposited(clanId, cs.clansmanId, w, ir, wh, fi, tick);
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6846:./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5686:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.

docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6990:./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6825:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2787:docs/reviews/pr200-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7167:  `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol` `ResourcesDeposited`; `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol` `_resolveAction` / `_doDeposit`; ABI JSON.  
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6991:./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6854:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3684:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7000:./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6971:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4945:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:1691:+        (uint256 wood, uint256 iron, uint256 wheat, uint256 blueprint) = world.getMonumentUpgradeCost(0);

That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.

docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7037:./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7761:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7618:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1703:+        (wood, iron, wheat, blueprint) = world.getMonumentUpgradeCost(6);
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7038:./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7763:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7621:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1716:+        (uint256 woodCost, uint256 ironCost, uint256 wheatCost, uint256 blueprintCost) = world.getMonumentUpgradeCost(0);
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7050:./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7843:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8186:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3684:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.

docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7802:./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7072:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5553:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7895:   866	        emit ResourcesDeposited(clanId, cs.clansmanId, w, ir, wh, fi, tick);
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7803:./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7074:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5686:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.

docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7932:./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7223:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6854:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3684:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7963:./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7256:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7412:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6525:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.

That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.

docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8383:./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7991:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3684:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8414:./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8098:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6525:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.
docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8430:./docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8161:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8186:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3684:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:The new `_simulateSettleToTick` chain (`ClanWorld.sol:~1014-1505`) faithfully mirrors the storage-mutating settlement, but it **cannot emit events** (it's `view`). That means UIs/indexers that read `getDerivedClanState` or `getClanFullView` will see wall/base/monument upgrades, deposits, and resource yields **before** any matching `WallLevelChanged`, `BaseLevelChanged`, `MonumentLevelChanged`, `ResourcesGathered`, `ResourcesDeposited`, `ClanStarvationChanged`, or `MissionCompleted` event is logged. This was a pre-existing tradeoff for read-side simulation, but it introduces a new operational risk: any indexer (`apps/server/convex`) that reconciles state from events will silently disagree with the contract's view-function output until the next real `settleClan()` runs. **Recommendation:** add a comment block on the simulation cluster noting that derived state may "lead" the event log by up to ~200 ticks, and confirm the Convex indexer is event-driven (not view-poll-driven) to avoid double-counting.

docs/reviews/pr199-r3-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8809:./docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:231:- **Spec compliance on §8 cost tables is line-perfect** — wall L1=20w, L2=35w, L3=30w+5i, L4=40w+10i, L5=50w+15i; base L2=40w+20wh through L5=100w+15i+50wh; monument L1=30w+20wh through L6=150w+20i+80wh, L7-10=200w+25i+100wh+1e18 blueprint. No drift here. Drift is on durations only (M4) and on ResourcesDeposited event shape (M7).
